question_text: Jupiter takes approximately 12 Earth years to orbit the Sun, while Mercury takes approximately 0.24 Earth years. Which statement correctly compares their orbital periods?

options.label: A
options.text: Jupiter orbits the Sun 50 times faster than Mercury

options.label: B
options.text: Mercury completes about 50 orbits for every 1 orbit of Jupiter

options.label: C
options.text: Mercury takes longer to orbit the Sun than Jupiter

options.label: D
options.text: Both planets take the same time to orbit the Sun

correct_answer: B) Mercury completes about 50 orbits for every 1 orbit of Jupiter
explanation: To compare orbital periods, we divide Jupiter's period by Mercury's: 12 ÷ 0.24 = 50. This means Mercury orbits the Sun 50 times in the time Jupiter completes just 1 orbit, because Mercury is closer to the Sun and travels faster.
